highlight.title: How AI is Rewiring Team Psychological Safety
highlight.summary: New research reveals that AI integration is creating predictable patterns of team dysfunction, eroding trust and second-guessing in ways traditional team effectiveness principles can address. Harvard's Amy Edmondson and 3M's Jayshree Seth show how leaders must actively foster psychological safety in human-AI collaborative environments.

resources.title: The 5Cs Framework for Human+AI Leadership
resources.why_now: DDI's research identifies five critical capabilities—Connection, Conscience, Creativity, Clarity, and Curiosity—that give leaders the edge in AI-driven environments. With 88% of organizations deploying AI but struggling with individual-level use cases, this framework helps leaders define their unique human value.

resources.title: Beyond Quarterly Thinking: The WEF Leadership Impact Scorecard
resources.why_now: Young global leaders propose a practical tool for measuring leadership impact across generations—assessing whether senior teams became stronger, whether initiatives deliver results five years later, and whether decisions expand future options. Essential as short-term metrics increasingly fail complex challenges.

resources.title: The Sphere of Influence Mapping Exercise
resources.why_now: With 71% of leaders reporting increased stress and spans of control rapidly expanding, this practical development tool helps leaders visualize how their decisions cascade through processes, platforms, and partners—addressing the reality that individual leadership choices now scale across entire systems.

resources.title: Team Psychological Safety as a Perishable Resource
resources.why_now: Springer research shows psychological safety doesn't arise passively from time together but requires active team processes: connecting, clarifying, supporting, and performing. This challenges assumptions about remote work and provides actionable insights for maintaining team effectiveness in hybrid environments.

claude_take: The most striking finding is how AI is exposing fundamental leadership weaknesses rather than replacing leaders—creating an urgency around emotional intelligence and human connection skills. While everyone talks about AI transformation, the real competitive advantage lies in leaders who can maintain psychological safety and human-centered decision-making as their teams become increasingly hybrid human-AI entities.
